elements.sent-2411-001: We analyze the many-body Hamiltonian describing a mobile impurity immersed in a Bose-Einstein condensate (BEC).
elements.sent-2411-002: Using exact unitary transformations and rigorous error estimates, we show the validity of the Bogoliubov-Fröhlich Hamiltonian for the Bose polaron in the regime of moderately strong, repulsive interactions with a dilute BEC.
elements.sent-2411-003: Moreover, we calculate analytically the universal logarithmic correction to the ground state energy that arises from an impurity mediated phonon-phonon interaction.
elements.sent-2411-004: The concept of a polaron, a quasi-particle formed through the interaction of an impurity with a medium, is ubiquitous in condensed matter physics~\cite{alexandrov2010, grusdt2016c .}
elements.sent-2411-005: Among the many examples, the Bose polaron stands out by the degree of control over the interactions allowed by cold atomic gases.
elements.sent-2411-006: For this reason, it is seen as a possible platform for the simulation of polaron physics in analogous systems, and in particular allows to access the large-coupling regime~\cite{Joergensen2016, Hu2016, yan2020bose .}
elements.sent-2411-007: The system also serves a similar role for theory, as a many-body quantum system whose constituents are simple enough that one can understand its complex behavior and effective degrees of freedom from first principles and has thus attracted considerable attention~\cite{tempere2009,li2014, grusdt2015, christensen2015,grusdt2016d, grusdt2017, drescher2019, ichmoukhamedov2019, levinsen2021,massignan2021, seetharam-21, ichmoukhamedov2022, mostaan2023 .}
elements.sent-2411-008: For the same reason, the rigorous justification of the effective description of fluctuations in dilute Bose gases by Bogoliubov phonons is an active area of mathematical research~\cite{RodSch-09,YauYin-09,GriMacMar-10,GriMacMar-11,Sei-11,LewNamSch-15,LewNamSerSol-15,BocCenSch-17,BocBreCenSch-18,FouSol-20,FouSol-23,NamTri-23,BreSchSch-22, HabHaiNamSeiTri-23,caraci-24 , with recent results also considering impurities~\cite{MySe-20, LaPi22, LaTr24 .}}
elements.sent-2411-009: Polarons are often modeled by the Fröhlich Hamiltonian, in which the impurity interacts with the field of low-energy excitations of the medium by linear coupling.
elements.sent-2411-010: However, the validity of this model for the Bose polaron has been questioned~\cite{christensen2015 .}
elements.sent-2411-011: Given the importance of the Bose polaron as a model system, it is crucial to understand precisely the regime of validity of the Fr\"ohlich Hamiltonian and control the modelling errors.
elements.sent-2411-012: In this letter we give conditions under which the Fröhlich Hamiltonian provides a good approximation to the full many-body spectrum of a dilute Bose gas with an impurity, and provide rigorous error estimates.
elements.sent-2411-013: This allows the model to be used with a great deal of confidence, and certifies the analogies to systems with a similar Hamiltonian.
elements.sent-2411-014: We find that the total energy of the system has a logarithmic correction to the mean-field terms that is still universal, i.e., depends on the interaction only via the impurity-boson scattering length.
elements.sent-2411-015: Compared to similar terms in the pure Bose gas~\cite{wu1959, sawada-59, HuPi-59, caraci2023 , it might be more accessible experimentally, by tuning the impurity-boson interaction.}
elements.sent-2411-016: We consider an impurity interacting with a dilute Bose gas ($\rho_\mathrm{B a_\BB^3\ll1$; $\rho_\mathrm{B}$ is the boson density, $a_\BB$ the boson-boson scattering length) at zero temperature.}
elements.sent-2411-017: Both the boson-boson and boson-impurity interactions are assumed to be repulsive.
elements.sent-2411-018: In this situation, the relevant low-energy excitations are the Bogoliubov phonons.
elements.sent-2411-019: In order for the Fröhlich Hamiltonian to provide a good approximation, the impurity-boson interaction should not be so strong as to deplete the Bose-Einstein condensate.
elements.sent-2411-020: On the other hand, it should also be strong enough that the impurity-phonon interaction cannot simply be neglected.
elements.sent-2411-021: This corresponds to the regime in which the dimensionless polaronic coupling, which we express in terms of the scattering lengths and the healing length $\xi = 1/\sqrt{8\pi \rho_{\BB a_\BB}$ of the Bose gas as \begin{equation}\alpha= a_\IB^2 \xi \rho_\mathrm{B}=\frac{a_\IB^2}{8 \pi a_\BB \xi}\end{equation} is of order one (definitions in~\cite{tempere2009, grusdt2016c differ by a factor of $8\pi$).}}
elements.sent-2411-022: That is, the impurity-boson interaction has considerably longer range than the boson-boson interaction.
elements.sent-2411-023: In experiments probing the strong-coupling regime of the Bose polaron~\cite{Joergensen2016, Hu2016, yan2020bose , this corresponds to moderate interactions.}
elements.sent-2411-024: We now give a concrete model realizing the situation described above and for which we will derive an asymptotic expansion of the eigenvalues in $\rho_\mathrm{B a_\BB^3\ll1$, featuring the Bogoliubov-Fröhlich Hamiltonian.}
elements.sent-2411-025: We consider a single impurity interacting with $N$ bosons in a box of size $L=\xi$ with periodic boundary conditions at zero temperature.
elements.sent-2411-026: In units where $\hbar=\xi=1$, and also the impurity mass is $M=1$, the Hamiltonian is \begin{equation}\hat H = \tfrac12 \hat \bP^2 + \sum_{j=1}^N \frac{\hat \bp_j^2}{2m} + \sum_{1\leq i<j\leq N} V_\BB(\bx_i-\bx_j) + \sum_{j=1}^N V_\IB(\bX-\bx_j),\end{equation} where uppercase letters denote quantities pertaining to the impurity, lower case letters those of the bosons.
elements.sent-2411-027: As $8\pi a_\BB/\xi=N^{-1}$, we write \begin{equation}\label{eq:VBB} V_\BB(\bx)=N^2 \tilde V_\BB(N \bx),\end{equation} where now $\tilde a_\BB= N a_\BB=1/8\pi$ (cf.
elements.sent-2411-028: Similarly, $a_\IB/\xi=\sqrt{\alpha N^{-1/2}$ and we write \begin{equation}\label{eq:VIB} V_\IB(\bX)=N \tilde V_\IB(\sqrt N \bX)\end{equation} with $\tilde a_\IB=\sqrt{N a_\IB=\sqrt \alpha$.}}
elements.sent-2411-029: We consider $\tilde V_\BB$, $\tilde V_\IB$ to be fixed while we make asymptotic expansions in $N$.
elements.sent-2411-030: We will compare the Hamiltonian $\hat H$ with the effective Fröhlich Hamiltonian, given by \begin{equation}\label{eq:HBF} \hat H_BF = \tfrac12 \hat \bP^2 +\sum_{\bk\neq0} \omega_k b_\bk^\dagger b_{\bk} + \frac{2\pi \sqrt\alpha}{\mu}\sum_{\bk\neq 0}e^{-i\bX \cdot\bk} \sqrt{\frac{k^2}{2m\omega_k}} (b_{\bk}^\dagger + b_{-\bk})\end{equation} where $\mu^{-1 =1+m^{-1}$ is the reduced mass, $b_\bk^\dagger$, $b_{\bk}$ create and annihilate Bogoliubov phonons, whose dispersion relation is \begin{equation}\omega_k=\sqrt{k^2/(2m)(k^2/2m + 8\pi N a_{\BB}/m)}=\tfrac{1}{2m}\sqrt{ k^4 + 2k^2}.\end{equation}.}
elements.sent-2411-031: Note that the expression~\eqref{eq:HBF requires renormalization~\cite{La20Bog , so what we really mean by $\hat H_\mathrm{BF}$ is the renormalized version of~\eqref{eq:HBF (constructed in~\cite{La20Bog,LaTr24 and explained below).}}}}
elements.sent-2411-032: Then, as $N= \xi/(8\pi a_\BB)\to \infty$ the ground state energy in units of $1/\xi^2$ is \begin{equation}E_0 =\frac{1}{4m} N + \frac{2\pi}{\mu}\sqrt\alpha N^{1/2} \quad -\frac{16 \pi \alpha^2}{\mu}\Big(\mu^{-1}\arcsin \mu -\sqrt{1- \mu^2} \Big) \log N + O(1).\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-033: Moreover, the excited eigenvalues satisfy \begin{equation}E_n-E_0=E_n^\mathrm{BF}-E_0^\mathrm{BF} + \mathcal{O}(N^{-\delta})\end{equation} for some $\delta>0$.
elements.sent-2411-034: The following term is the analogue for the boson-impurity interaction $8\pi a_\IB \rho_\mathrm{B \rho_\mathrm{I L^3$.}}
elements.sent-2411-035: The term of order $\log N$ arises from an effective phonon-phonon interaction mediated by the impurity, and thus vanishes in the limit of a heavy impurity, $M\to \infty$.
elements.sent-2411-036: Such a term has been observed in the context of the Fröhlich-Hamiltonian~\cite{grusdt2015, La20Bog , and our study shows that it is the leading correction to the mean-field approximation.}
elements.sent-2411-037: Similar terms can be found in the expansion of the energy in $a_\BB/\xi$ (without impurity)~\cite{wu1959, sawada-59, HuPi-59, caraci2023 , and in $a_\IB/\xi$~\cite{christensen2015 , but in our parameter regime these are of lower order ($N^{-1}\log N$, resp.. $N^{-1/2 \log N$).}}}
elements.sent-2411-038: This energy shift may thus be much easier to detect experimentally than the corresponding terms in the pure Bose gas, which so far is only theoretical.
elements.sent-2411-039: Indeed, the shift is even larger than the Lee-Huang-Yang~\cite{LeeHuangYang-57 term, which is of order one in our units and has been observed~\cite{papp2008, navon2011, skov2021, lavoine2021 .}}
elements.sent-2411-040: The contribution to $E_0$ of order $\log N$ can be understood as a three-body effect due to the interaction of the impurity and two bosons, like the similar term for the pure Bose gas~\cite{wu1959, sawada-59, HuPi-59 .}
elements.sent-2411-041: We now give a heuristic derivation of its value by considering only the 2+1\textendash body problem.
elements.sent-2411-042: An account in the $N$-body problem, and its relation to the renormalization of the Bogoliubov-Fröhlich Hamiltonian will be given later.
elements.sent-2411-043: In relative coordinates $\br_i=\bX-\bx_i$, $i=1,2$, the Hamiltonian with $V_\BB=0$ is \begin{equation}\hat{H}_{2+1}= -\tfrac1{2\mu}(\Delta_{\br_1}+\Delta_{\br_2})+ \nabla_{\br_1}\cdot\nabla_{\br_2} + V_\IB(\br_1) + V_\IB(\br_2).\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-044: Given the short range $a_\IB =N^{-1/2}\sqrt{\alpha}$ of the interaction, two-body effects will dominate and we make the ansatz $\Psi(\br_1, \br_2)=f(\br_1)f(\br_2)$ for a low-energy state, with $f$ normalized, periodic.
elements.sent-2411-045: Calculating the energy yields \begin{equation}\label{eq:2-body min} \langle \Psi, \hat{H}_{2+1} \Psi \rangle=-\frac{1}{\mu} \int \overline{f(\br)} \Delta f(\br) + 2 \int V_\IB(\br)f(\br)^2 d^3r,\end{equation} as the term with $\nabla_{\br_1}\cdot\nabla_{\br_2}$ integrates to zero.
elements.sent-2411-046: The minimizer $f$ of the right hand side solves a periodized version of the zero-energy scattering equation ($f\propto 1+\phi_\IB$ from~\eqref{eq:phiIB ).}
elements.sent-2411-047: Inserting this into~\eqref{eq:2-body min gives the leading contribution to the energy $2 E_{\text{2-bd }\sim 4\pi N^{-1/2}\sqrt \alpha/\mu$.}}
elements.sent-2411-048: In order to obtain a more precise result, we refine the ansatz by taking into account 3-body effects, $\Psi(\br_1,\br_2)=f(\br_1)f(\br_2) \Phi(\br_1,\br_2)$, where $\Phi$ is normalized and differs significantly from one only where $r_1, r_2\lesssim a_\IB$.
elements.sent-2411-049: Then, the mixed derivatives no longer integrate to zero and play the role of an effective potential \begin{equation}\label{eq:Veff} V_\text{eff}(\br_1,\br_2)= \nabla f(\br_1) \cdot\nabla f(\br_2).\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-050: Minimizing in $\Phi$, the three-body contribution is a generalized scattering energy of $V_\mathrm{eff}$~\cite{nam2023 .}
elements.sent-2411-051: It evaluates approximately to \begin{equation}E_{3-bd} \approx \sum_{\bk_1,\bk_2} \frac{(\bk_1\cdot \bk_2)^2 |\hat f(\bk_1)|^2 |\hat f(\bk_2)|^2 }{\frac{1}{2\mu}(k_1^2+k_2^2)+ \bk_1\cdot \bk_2 } \approx 32\pi \alpha^2 \mu^{-1}\Big(\mu^{-1}\arcsin \mu -\sqrt{1- \mu^2} \Big) N^{-2}\log N.\notag\end{equation} The term in the expansion of the $N$-body energy is obtained by multiplying $E_{\text{3-bd }$ by $N^2/2$, or the number of boson pairs.}
elements.sent-2411-052: To compare the $N$-body and the Bogoliubov-Fröhlich Hamiltonian we will conjugate $\hat H$ by a series of unitary transformations.

elements.sent-2411-065: The transformations $U_q$ and $U_c$ take care of creation and annihilation of the so-called hard and soft pairs of bosons with large momenta (the sum of which is either zero or small) and the map $U_W$ deals with the large momentum scattering between the bosons and the impurity.

elements.sent-2411-085: At this stage, ignoring cubic and quartic contributions, the resulting Hamiltonian is unitarily equivalent to $\hat H_{\mathrm{Bog }$ cut off at momentum $N^{\tau}$, via a Weyl transformation similar to $U_W^\dagger$.}
elements.sent-2411-086: The renormalization at the order $N^\tau$ its ultraviolet divergence is performed again via a Bogoliubov and a Weyl transformation, which are however not perturbations of the identity.
elements.sent-2411-087: To diagonalize the boson Hamiltonian, we define the usual phonon operators \begin{equation}\label{eq:b-operators} b_\bk=u_k a_\bk + v_k a^\dagger_\bk,\quad b^\dagger_\bk=u_k a^\dagger_\bk + v_k a_\bk,\end{equation} with $u_k=\cosh(\theta_k)$, $v_k=\sinh(\theta_k)$ for $\theta_k= \tfrac12\log(k^2/(2m\omega_k))$ if $k<N^\tau$; otherwise $\theta_k=0$.
elements.formula-2411-022: \begin{equation}\label{eq:b-operators} b_\bk=u_k a_\bk + v_k a^\dagger_\bk,\quad b^\dagger_\bk=u_k a^\dagger_\bk + v_k a_\bk,\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-088: The Bogoliubov Hamiltonian becomes $\sum_{\bk \omega_k b^\dagger_\bk b_\bk$ plus its ground state energy of order $N^\tau$, which combines with the scalar term created by $U_q$ to yield the usual Lee-Huang-Yang \cite{LeeHuangYang-57 correction term, which is of order one in our regime.}}
elements.sent-2411-089: The linear coupling between impurity and the bosons changes to \begin{equation}\eqref{eq:VIB-lin-ren} \mapsto \frac{2\pi \sqrt\alpha}{\mu}\sum_{0<k<N^\tau} \sqrt{\frac{k^2}{2m\omega_k}} e^{-i\bX \cdot\bk}b_\bk^\dagger+\hc\,.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-090: The transformation also creates terms of the form $\sum_{\bk,\bl \hat V_\IB(\bk)u_{\bk+\bl v_\bl b^\dagger_{\bk+\bl b^\dagger_\bl$ from the quadratic interaction term~\eqref{eq:VBB-quad , which are responsible for the logarithmic correction to the energy found in~\cite{christensen2015 .}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-091: In our case this is of the lower order $N^{-1/2 \log N$, due to the decay of $v_k\sim k^{-2}$ for large $k$ (compare~\cite[Lem.4.25]{LaTr24 ).}}
elements.sent-2411-092: We can now finish regularizing the linear terms (\ref{eq:VIB-lin-ren ) with another Weyl transform, taking into account the correct dispersion relation $\omega$ for low-energy phonon-impurity scattering.}
elements.sent-2411-093: Its action is given by \begin{equation}\label{eq:Weyl2} b_\bk \mapsto b_\bk + e^{-i\bX\cdot\bk} \frac{2\pi \sqrt \alpha}{\mu(\tfrac12 k^2+ \omega_k)}\sqrt\frac{k^2}{2m\omega_k}.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-094: This extracts a scalar of order $N^{\tau}$ which combines with the one created by $U_W$, yielding again a contribution of order one.
elements.sent-2411-095: It also transforms the linear terms (\ref{eq:VIB-lin-ren ) to more regular quadratic terms, completing the ones in (\ref{eq:int-tau ).}}

elements.formula-2411-025: \begin{equation}U^\dagger \hat H U\approx\frac{1}{4m} N + \frac{2\pi}{\mu}\sqrt\alpha N^{1/2} + E + \hat K \label{eq:BF-int-cutoff} + \frac12 \sum_{k,\ell\neq 0} w(\bk)(\bk\cdot\bl)w(\bl)e^{-i\bX\cdot(\bk+\bl)} b^\dagger_\bk b^\dagger_\bl +\hc +\hat R, \notag\end{equation}
elements.formula-2411-026: \begin{equation}\hat K=\tfrac12 \hat P^2 + \sum_{\bk} \omega_k b^\dagger_\bk b_\bk+ \sum_{\bk,\bl\neq 0} \hat V_\IB(\bk) e^{-i\bX \cdot\bk} b^\dagger_{\bk+\bl} b_\bl , \notag\end{equation}
elements.formula-2411-027: \begin{equation}\hat R= \sum_{k,\ell\neq 0} w(\bk)(\bk\cdot\bl)w(\bl)e^{-i\bX\cdot(\bk+\bl)} b^\dagger_\bk b_{-\bl} + \sum_{k\neq0 } w(\bk) e^{-i\bX\cdot\bk} b_{\bk}^\dagger \hat P + \hc \,.\end{equation}
elements.sent-2411-097: The Hamiltonian~\eqref{eq:BF-int-cutoff can be renormalized non-perturbatively~\cite{La20Bog, LaTr24 .}}
elements.sent-2411-098: The terms responsible for the divergence are those creating or annihilating pairs of phonons.
elements.sent-2411-099: To deal with these, we set \begin{equation}G= -\frac{1}{2 \hat K} \sum_{k,\ell\neq 0} w(\bk)(\bk\cdot\bl)w(\bl)e^{-i\bX\cdot(\bk+\bl)} b^\dagger_{\bk}b^\dagger_{\bl},\end{equation} and rewrite the Hamiltonian as \begin{equation}(1-G^\dagger)\hat K (1-G)- G^\dagger \hat K G + \hat R,\end{equation} which eliminates the problematic interaction terms.
elements.sent-2411-100: The new term $G^\dagger \hat K G$ contains the divergent self energy.
elements.sent-2411-101: Putting this term into normal order, we find the scalar \begin{equation}\langle \varnothing |\hat G^\dagger \hat K^{-1} \hat G|\varnothing\rangle \approx\frac12\sum_{\bk,\bl\neq 0}\frac{w(\bk)^2w(\bl)^2(\bk\cdot\bl)^2 }{\frac12 |\bk+\bl|^2 + \omega_{k}+\omega_\ell}\end{equation} plus corrections of order one due to $\hat V_\IB(\bk)$.
elements.sent-2411-102: Evaluating the sum for large $N$ yields the $\log N$-term from Theorem~\ref{thm:main .}
elements.sent-2411-103: One easily confirms that $\langle \varnothing |\hat R^\dagger \hat K^{-1 \hat R|\varnothing\rangle$ is of order one, i.e, $\hat R$ is not singular.}
elements.sent-2411-104: The normal ordered term $\mathbin{: G^\dagger \hat K G\mathbin{:}$ no longer suffers from singularities and gives a finite operator also for $N=\infty$ (since it contains an additional factor of $\hat K^{-1}$).}
elements.sent-2411-105: This is also true for $\hat R$.
elements.sent-2411-106: We thus have a limiting operator for $N\to \infty$ that is unitarily equivalent to the renormalized Bogoliubov-Fr\"ohlich Hamiltonian via the inverse of the Weyl transformation~\eqref{eq:Weyl2 .}
elements.sent-2411-107: Note that the term with $\hat V_\IB(\bk)$ disappears from $\hat K$ in the limit.
elements.sent-2411-108: This term gives a global energy shift order one, but does not change the differences of eigenvalues.
elements.sent-2411-109: The proof of Theorem~\ref{thm:main relies on min-max formulas which require bounds on various error terms accumulated in the calculations.}
elements.sent-2411-110: As we have explained, these are small relative to the kinetic energies, and the positive interaction terms~\eqref{eq:VBB-quart ,~\eqref{eq:VIB-quad .}}
elements.sent-2411-111: They may thus be neglected for a lower bound on the energy.
elements.sent-2411-112: An upper bound is obtained by constructing trial states using the eigenvectors of the Bogoliubov-Fr\"ohlich Hamiltonian and the unitary transformations.
elements.sent-2411-113: We have obtained an expansion of the energy for a dilute Bose gas coupled to an impurity with effective coupling $\alpha\sim 1$.
elements.sent-2411-114: By analyzing precisely the renormalization of the model parameters due to the emergence of a point-potential, we have shown the presence of a correction due to an effective three-body interaction.
elements.sent-2411-115: We have also established that the excitation spectrum is accurately described by the Bogoliubov-Fröhlich Hamiltonian.
elements.sent-2411-116: This work was funded by the French National Research Agency (ANR) and the German Research Foundation (DFG) through the projects (MaBoP, ANR-23-CE40-0025; PI 1114/8-1) and CRC-TRR 352 (Project-ID 470903074).. J.L.
elements.sent-2411-117: The concept of a polaron, a quasi-particle formed through the interaction of an impurity with a medium, is ubiquitous in condensed matter physics~\cite{alexandrov2010, grusdt2016c, grusdt2024 .}
elements.sent-2411-118: The system also serves a similar role for theory, as a many-body quantum system whose constituents are simple enough that one can understand its complex behavior and effective degrees of freedom from first principles and has thus attracted considerable attention~\cite{tempere2009,rath2013, li2014, grusdt2015, christensen2015,grusdt2016d, grusdt2017, drescher2019, ichmoukhamedov2019, levinsen2021,massignan2021, seetharam-21, ichmoukhamedov2022, mostaan2023 .}
